title: /analytics is live — every signal PointCast already has, on one page
dek: Mike said 'start the analytics page where we are measuring interaction and feedback, be expansive.' cc shipped /analytics — 10 sections pulling from the compute ledger, the git branch attribution JSON, the blocks filesystem, the ship queue, the sprint recaps, the live presence + drum + tank + polls endpoints, and the agent-ready surface. No third-party analytics. No pixels. Every number is either in the repo or from an endpoint this site serves.
body:
**Starting shape.** `/analytics` is a single long page with 10 sections plus a stat-grid header. The design brief was 'be expansive' — cc translated that to 'include every signal PointCast already has, don't add new instrumentation.' The page is the receipt for what's already measurable; it's not a new measurement layer.

**The 10 sections.**

**§1 Ships.** Compute-ledger rows. Total count, last-24h count, last-7d count. A 14-day bar chart of ships-per-day (built from the ledger's `at` timestamps). Three side-by-side splits: ships by kind (sprint / block / brief / ops / editorial), ships by signature (shy / modest / healthy / heavy), ship-queue state (queued / shipped / deferred / in-flight / skipped).

**§2 Collaborators.** Per-collab table with four columns: ledger ships (hand-curated), heaviest signature, branch commits (from `docs/notes/git-branch-attribution.json`, the file the git-branch mapper produces), briefs queued (counted from filenames under `docs/briefs/`). Totals row at the bottom. This is the clearest 'who's doing what' surface the site has ever had.

**§3 Blocks.** Count + reading-time total + per-channel breakdown + per-type breakdown. Pulls from `src/content/blocks/*.json` at build time.

**§4 Presence (live).** Three live counters (humans, agents, total sessions) reading `/api/presence/snapshot` every 30s. The first non-compile-time section — the DO handles the real-time part.

**§5 Interaction.** Six live/semi-live counters: lifetime drum beats (from `/api/drum`), fish in tank (from `/api/tank/state`), polls on home, TV shows (12), leaderboards (8), /lab prototypes (4). The three endpoint-backed numbers refresh every 60s.

**§6 Feedback.** Four write-endpoint chips: `/api/ping`, `/api/drop`, `/api/feedback`, `/cos`. Each with a short explainer. Anyone (human or agent) can ping, drop, feedback, or use /cos — all CORS-open, all rate-limited per session, none requires auth.

**§7 Agent-ready.** The full list of what AI agents can discover: 3 `/.well-known/*` Pages Functions (OAuth authz server, OIDC, protected resource, agent-passport), 10 agent skills declared in `/.well-known/agent-skills/index.json`, 7 WebMCP tools registered via `navigator.modelContext.provideContext()` on every page. Links to `/for-agents`, `/for-nodes`, `/rfc/compute-ledger-v0`.

**§8 Cadence.** Sprint recap count (94+), total briefs queued across collabs, queued ships, shipped-from-queue count. Narration-support for the 'how fast is this building' question.

**§9 Last ten ships.** A newspaper-style table of the 10 most recent ledger rows — timestamp, collab, kind, title-as-link, signature. For the 'what just happened' reader.

**§10 Not measured (on purpose).** Six explicit non-measurements. No page-view analytics. No cookies. No fingerprinting. No A/B framework. No conversion funnels. No third-party SDKs. This section is the editorial philosophy line — the analytics page is NOT a tracking page.

**What the stat grid shows at the top.** Ledger entries total, blocks total, sprint recaps total, collab count, branch commits total, agent-surfaces total (= WebMCP tools + agent skills + well-known functions). Six big numbers, all live at build time.

**Data sources the page uses.** `src/lib/compute-ledger.ts` (ledger), `src/lib/ship-queue.ts` (queue state), `src/lib/sprint-recap.ts` (sprint recap reader), filesystem walks of `src/content/blocks/`, `docs/briefs/`, `public/.well-known/`, `functions/.well-known/`, `src/components/WebMCPTools.astro` (grep for tool count). And at runtime: `/api/presence/snapshot`, `/api/drum`, `/api/tank/state`, `/polls.json`. If any endpoint is down the counter shows `—`; the rest of the page still works.

**Why 'receipt over dashboard'.** A dashboard implies a desired direction (KPIs should go up and to the right). A receipt implies honesty — this is what shipped, this is what didn't, no spin. PointCast doesn't have conversion funnels or A/B tests; analytics-as-funnel would be a mismatch. Analytics-as-receipt is an honest extension of the 'compute is the currency, the ledger is the receipt' framing from block 0330 and the RFC v0.

**What federating nodes can copy.** Every data source above is either in the repo or at a public HTTP endpoint. A peer running the same stack can implement the same page in a weekend by: (a) reading their own `src/lib/compute-ledger.ts`-equivalent, (b) reading their own `git log`, (c) calling their own presence / write endpoints. The Compute Ledger RFC v0 at `/rfc/compute-ledger-v0` describes the schema; the analytics page is what the schema feels like when you render it.

**Future surfaces.** Today's page is v0. Likely v0.1 additions: per-channel time-series (when did GDN ship most?), drum leaderboard embedded inline, tank roster snapshot with nounId gallery, an 'agents active right now' section that cross-references the presence DO with the WebMCP-tool-call count (needs a server-side log the WebMCP script doesn't have yet). Each is a small extension.

**Live at** [pointcast.xyz/analytics](https://pointcast.xyz/analytics). Reading time for the whole page: ~3 minutes. Updates on every deploy (build-time data) + every 30s (live endpoints). If you want the raw JSON, `/compute.json` carries the ledger and the /analytics page itself is meant to be bookmarked + revisited.
author: mh+cc
source: Mike 17:15 PT: 'ok go, and lets start the analytics page where we are measuring interaction and feedback, be expansive.' cc shipped src/pages/analytics.astro (~500 lines including styles + inline script for live fetches). 10 sections + stat grid + live counters. Pulls from ledger, ship-queue, sprint-recap, blocks filesystem, git-branch-attribution JSON, well-known surface, WebMCP tools, and live /api/presence + /api/drum + /api/tank + /polls endpoints. Author = mh+cc.

companions.label: Compute Ledger RFC v0 — the framing /analytics extends
Federated compute — compute is the currency
